LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT PAUL LEININGER'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE. THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING. DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE AGREEMENT WAS "MADE" IN SAN FRANCISCO WITHIN THE MEANING OF CCP 1292. "[T]HE CONTRACT WILL BE DEEMED TO HAVE BEEN CONSUMMATED AND BECOME BINDING AT THE TIME AND PLACE WHERE THE LAST ACT NECESSARY TO ITS VALIDITY WAS PERFORMED. ..." (BRAUNSTEIN V. SUP. CRT. (1964) 225 CAL.APP.2D 691, 697.) MR. LEININGER MADE CHANGES TO THE ATTORNEY/CLIENT FEE AGREEMENT, SENT IT BACK TO COUNSEL, AND COUNSEL EXECUTED THE AGREEMENT FOUR DAYS AFTER MR. LEININGER SIGNED THE AGREEMENT. COUNSEL ACCEPTED MR. LEININGER'S COUNTER-OFFER IN SAN FRANCISCO. (THORN DEC., PAR. 4.) IN ADDITION, THE AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE WAS TO BE PERFORMED IN SAN FRANCISCO. COURTS LOOK AT AGREEMENTS TO ARBITRATE AS SEVERABLE FROM THE UNDERLYING AGREEMENT WHEN DETERMINING WHETHER THE AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE WAS PROCURED BY FRAUD. THE SAME ANALYSIS APPLIES HERE AND THE AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE CLEARLY SHOWS THAT IT WAS TO BE PERFORMED IN SAN FRANCISCO. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR NO. 6576, E-MAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG

Notice Of Continued Hearing On Verified Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration
Off Calendar Dec-14-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

PETITIONER SCHAPIRO-THORN, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION's Continued Hearing On Verified Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration is Off calendar per stipulated order filed December 11, 2017.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D302)

Notice Of Continued Hearing On Verified Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration
Off Calendar Nov-15-2017 Continued To Dec-14-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PETITIONER SCHAPIRO-THORN, INC.'S Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration is CONTINUED to December 14, 2017 at 9:30 AM in Department 302/ Law and Motion. Respondent Paul Leininger may file and serve substantive opposition papers to the petition by November 30, 2017. Schapiro-Thorn may file and serve reply papers by December 7, 2017.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)

MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
Off Calendar Nov-15-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

RESPONDENT PAUL LEININGER'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE is denied. The court finds that the agreement was "made" in San Francisco within the meaning of CCP 1292. "[T]he contract will be deemed to have been consummated and become binding at the time and place where the last act necessary to its validity was performed. ..." (Braunstein v. Sup. Crt. (1964) 225 Cal.App.2d 691, 697.) Mr. Leininger made changes to the attorney/client fee agreement, sent it back to counsel, and counsel executed the agreement four days after Mr. Leininger signed the agreement. Counsel accepted Mr. Leininger's counter-offer in San Francisco. (Thorn Dec., par. 4.) In addition, the agreement to arbitrate was to be performed in San Francisco. Courts look at agreements to arbitrate as severable from the underlying agreement when determining whether the agreement to arbitrate was procured by fraud. The same analysis applies here and the agreement to arbitrate clearly shows that it was to be performed in San Francisco. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)

Notice Of Continued Hearing On Verified Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration
